title: The Chewing The Cud.
content:
When well we speak and nothing do that's good,
    We not divide the hoof, but chew the cud;
    But when good words by good works have their proof,
    We then both chew the cud and cleave the hoof.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "The Chewing The Cud."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
